description: You are a public health researcher analyzing a 1978 influenza outbreak in a boarding school. Using the case data in '~/Documents/GeoGebra/data/flu_1978.csv', calibrate a dynamic SIR model (S' = -beta * S * I / N, I' = beta * S * I / N - gamma * I, R' = gamma * I) where N = 763, S(0)=762, I(0)=1, and R(0)=0. Fit the model's infected curve to the observed data for the period t=0 to t=15 by determining appropriate values for the transmission rate (beta) and recovery rate (gamma). Ensure the infected curve is visually distinct from the data points. Save the project to '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/sir_analysis.ggb'.
